title: CompletionVerdict
description: Output of the rebar.llm completion-verification operation (`rebar verify-completion <id>`): a PASS/FAIL verdict that a ticket's completion criteria (acceptance/success/close/completion criteria, definitions of done; for bugs, resolution) are demonstrably met by the implementation. SHAPE-ONLY: the FAIL⇔findings invariant and verdict normalization are enforced by the operation's `reconcile_verdict` (code), not the schema, because the runner validates this schema BEFORE the operation can repair a sloppy model turn. `verdict` is `string` (not a strict enum) so the operation can normalize casing. Each FAIL finding reuses the canonical `finding` shape with an added `criterion` (the specific requirement that failed). The MCP `verify_completion` tool returns this shape as a plain dict but advertises no outputSchema (live LLM call — a documented NO_SCHEMA_EXEMPT).

verdict.description: PASS or FAIL (normalized by the operation; not a strict enum so model casing can be repaired).

findings.description: One finding per FAILING criterion (empty on PASS). Reuses the canonical `finding` shape; the failing requirement rides on `finding.criterion` (an additional property). A finding may also carry an optional `remediation` string — a per-finding next move (for an operator-attested criterion judged NOT MET: record proof as a ticket comment/artifact naming the reference, observed outcome, and when) — distinct from the top-level generic `remediation`.

summary.description: Optional verifier summary; carries the no-explicit-criteria PASS rationale when applicable.

remediation.description: Guidance carried on a FAIL verdict (set by the operation's `reconcile_verdict`): how to satisfy the unmet criteria, including documenting evidence that meets a requirement as a comment on the ticket — the verifier reads a ticket's comments, so recorded evidence is taken into account on the next verification. Absent on PASS (nothing to remediate).

certifiable.description: Whether the close may be CERTIFIED (signed). False iff a direct child is closed-but-uncertified (force-closed) — the parent may close but not certify, because certification propagates: an unattested descendant leaves the subtree unattested. Absent/true on the common path. Does not affect the PASS/FAIL verdict (the parent's own criteria stand); the close gate reads it to close without signing.

model.description: Model id used (null for non-LLM/fake runners).

trace_id.description: Langfuse trace id when tracing is enabled; null otherwise.
